dead_ends.reason: Uses wrong corrSum formula (individual vs cumulative)
N0(p) > 0 for most primes p|d with cumulative formula
dead_ends.constraint: CRT interference needed, not single-prime blocking
Image is too sparse for exp sum equidistribution

dead_ends.reason: Collision count too high due to sparse image

dead_ends.approach: Inertia of d in number field
dead_ends.reason: d always splits (2 is always a root of X^S-3^k mod d)
dead_ends.constraint: By construction: 2^S ≡ 3^k mod d, so 2 is a root

dead_ends.approach: Number field norm for all k
dead_ends.reason: N(P_σ(α)) can be divisible by d for k ≥ 5
dead_ends.constraint: Other conjugates absorb the d factor, not the real one

dead_ends.reason: corrSum mod d not always in <2,3> for k ≥ 4
dead_ends.constraint: Works for k=3,5 but not universal
Proper divisors of d are achieved as gcd values

partial_successes.what_works: Proves k=3,4 completely (N coprime to d for prime d)
Some primes p|d have v_p(corrSum)=0 for all σ
partial_successes.what_fails: Not all k have a blocking prime

partial_successes.what_works: ALL free solutions have crossings; sorting correction ≠ 0
partial_successes.what_fails: Verified computationally, not yet proved for all k

partial_successes.what_works: Every individual swap correction is nonzero

open_questions.question: Why is ρ=2/3 never a root of the correction polynomial Q_δ?
open_questions.context: Vandermonde analysis shows ρ avoids all root sets

open_questions.question: Can Baker's theorem prove ord_d(2) > S-k for all k?
open_questions.context: Baker gives ord ≥ exp(c·√(log d)), need > S-k ≈ 0.585k

open_questions.question: Is d always the UNIQUE unachieved divisor of gcd(corrSum,d)?
open_questions.context: Verified for k=3..12, fails for k=7,8,11 (extra unachieved)

open_questions.question: Can the Catalan-Mihailescu connection be exploited?
